description_long: This is a tall, ornate stone monument featuring a vertical column with a sculpted figure at the top, likely representing a person or symbolic form. The base is broad and stepped, with detailed engravings and inscriptions visible on the front face, possibly commemorative text. Flanking the lower section of the monument are sculpted animal figures, possibly lions, which add to the grandeur and symbolic significance of the piece. The stone appears weathered, with a mix of lighter and darker tones, suggesting exposure to the elements. The monument sits on a square plinth that extends outward, with some irregularities and a visible hole in the base platform. The overall design is formal and commemorative, typical of public memorials found in parks or plazas.
description: A tall, ornate stone monument with a sculpted figure on top, flanked by animal figures and detailed inscriptions on a broad stepped base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main inscription panel, flanked by two animal sculptures and the central vertical column topped by a figure.
description_view_1: This side view highlights one of the animal sculptures in profile and the stepped base, with less visibility of the inscription.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plainer side of the monument with a smaller relief panel and both animal sculptures visible from behind.
description_view_3: This side view shows the other animal sculpture in profile and the monument's layered base, with minimal inscription detail visible.
attribute_subject_matter: commemorative monument with human and animal figures
attribute_location_context: The monument appears to be installed outdoors, likely in a public park or urban plaza, as suggested by the stone base and open surroundings.
